title: How to Find Funders and Partners
description: Webinar organized by Ghana chapter to educate others on how to find funders and partners for their activities.

title: UK Internet Governance Forum 2025
description: One-day event in London focused on “Building Digital Governance Together,” exploring key issues like digital cooperation, trust, innovation, and universal digital rights to shape the future of internet governance in the UK

title: ISOC PT 2025 Internet Research Prize
description: Delivery of the ISOC PT 2025 Internet Research Prize and panel discussion on the Age Control proposals

title: Universal Acceptance on a Multilingual Internet
description: This webinar will explore how Universal Acceptance forms the foundation for a truly multilingual Internet, the challenges of regional language support, multilingual DNS, inclusion in digital platforms, and the transformative role of technology, design, and policy in shaping linguistic equity online.

title: AI SIG "Wire-Free Sewer Vision" Hybrid Hackathon
description: A critical event designed to challenge how we monitor and maintain underground sewer systems using cutting-edge, autonomous technology.
organizer: ISOC Artificial Intelligence (AI) Special Interest Group

title: LINUX Para Uso Cotidiano
description: Este seminario introduce a Linux como una herramienta útil para el uso cotidiano. Se presentará el trasfondo operativo del sistema, las diferencias entre diversas distribuciones y cómo obtenerlas e instalarlas en distintas plataformas. Incluye demostraciones prácticas del entorno Linux y una sesión interactiva de preguntas y respuestas para aclarar dudas y guiar a quienes deseen comenzar a utilizar este sistema operativo en su vida diaria.

title: Budding Polymath
description: An intra-chapter event which showcases our young talent presenting on relevant topics

title: Meta-Layer Design Challenge
description: The Meta-Layer Design Challenge is a global, week-long design challenge exploring how learning, collaboration, and collective intelligence can be supported above the existing Web through a shared civic interface layer - the Meta-Layer. Rather than building new platforms, participants are invited to design concepts, interfaces, and governance models that operate across websites using overlays, shared context, trust signals, and community coordination. The challenge emphasizes human-centered design, interoperability, and public-interest outcomes. The Design Challenge is part of Meta-Layer Week (January 21–28, 2025), co-hosted with Learning Planet. Activities include live sessions, workshops, demonstrations, office hours, and a final showcase. Participants may submit conceptual designs, mockups, diagrams, governance proposals, or lightweight prototypes. No coding is required. Both a Student Track and an Open Track are offered, with shared judging criteria focused on creativity, feasibility, and relevance to collective learning and public-interest infrastructure. The challenge is relevant to Internet governance, digital public infrastructure, education, and community-driven innovation, and aligns with long-standing Internet Society interests in an open, trustworthy, and people-centered Internet.  Audience: Students, educators, designers, technologists, researchers, and community organizers
organizer: ISOC US San Francisco Bay Area Chapter

title: Art by the Internet
description: A talk by DY Kim

title: What does Digital Sovereignty mean for the Civil Society?
description: ISOC Switzerland Chapter’s yearly Public Policy Sessions event will be dedicated in 2026 on the concept of digital sovereignty, launching a long term campaign asking the question “What does digital sovereignty mean?” from the perspective of different actors. As a US-based organization defending digital rights across the world since the 90s, the Internet Society’s European Chapters can play a key role in making sure that the current wave of European claims on digital sovereignty will strengthen digital rights worldwide, while taking into account important challenges related to ecological sustainability, health, and resilience and key values like openness, privacy, and democracy. We are proud to welcome in Zurich 5 special guests with strong expertise on this topic from an academic and/or policy perspective, and active engagement with various civil society organizations, like ISOC Germany and Serbia, FSFE, Wikimedia, La Quadrature du Net. You will have also the chance to meet the growing ISOC Switzerland Chapter and its new home at the center of Zurich, the L200 space, and participate in the discussion!

title: South School on Internet Governance 2026
description: The South School on Internet Governance trains future thought leaders in Latin America and the Caribbean, preparing students to engage in international forums and shape the future of the internet in the region.

title: Universal Acceptance Day 2026- Regional Event
description: Register Idea Universal Acceptance is a global initiative focused on promoting the internationalization of national domain names (IDNs) and the email addresses associated with them (EAIs).

title: Fundamentos de IA Aplicada a Redes- Mayo
description: Este curso se divide en 4 secciones. Aprenderás a aplicar IA en redes, analizar demanda con datos geoespaciales, optimizar despliegues y liderar la adopción tecnológica.
organizer: ISOC Puerto Rico Chapter en colaboración con el Colegio de Ingenieros de Puerto Rico

title: IA para Planificación y Diseño de Redes- Junio
description: El curso tiene 4 secciones donde aprenderás a aplicar IA en redes, analizar demanda, optimizar despliegues y liderar la adopción tecnológica.
organizer: ISOC Puerto Rico Chapter en colaboración con el Colegio de Ingenieros de Puerto Rico

title: Uso Correcto de la Tecnología Digital
description: Este seminario ofrece una introducción práctica y accesible sobre cómo utilizar la tecnología digital de forma segura, responsable y eficiente.
organizer: ISOC Puerto Rico Chapter en colaboración con el Colegio de Ingenieros de Puerto Rico

title: Lenguajes de Programación de Computadoras
description: Este seminario ofrece una introducción clara y estructurada a los fundamentos de los lenguajes de programación, dirigido a quienes están interesados en comprender cómo funcionan las herramientas que impulsan el desarrollo de software.
organizer: ISOC Puerto Rico Chapter en colaboración con el Colegio de Ingenieros de Puerto Rico

title: Celebración del 30 Aniversario del Capítulo México
description: La celebración del trigésimo aniversario del Capítulo México de Internet Society reunirá a sus pioneros para recordar el origen del primer backbone de internet mexicano y reconocer su trayectoria y aportes.

title: Semana del Día de Internet
description: Del 18 al 23 de mayo de 2026, coordinamos una serie de eventos conmemorativos del Día de Internet en diversas sedes de la ciudad, incluyendo universidades y centros de eventos, abordando temáticas clave como Seguridad, Gobernanza y Conectividad.

title: UA TechBoost 2026 LACNOG
description: UA TechBoost reúne a líderes de ICANN, UNESCO, ISOC y LACNOG para impulsar estándares que reconozcan dominios y correos en cualquier idioma, asegurando que la transformación digital de América Latina llegue a todos.

title: Webinar sobre el proyecto MEDICIONES LAC
description: Un proyecto colaborativo para entender mediciones en Internet en LAC y trabajar en la implementación del DNSSEC.

title: Grupo de Mediciones LAC - ISOC Uruguay Chapter
description: Webinar organizado por Capítulo ISOC Uruguay para presentar avances del grupo a la comunidad regional.

title: Conoce a COMTELCA, Comisión Técnica Regional de Telecomunicaciones de CentroAmérica.
description: COMTELCA is SICA's specialized body coordinating telecommunications and ICT across Central America, uniting eight countries through a shared legal framework and binding resolutions.

title: Legislación de menores en redes sociales Caso: Costa Rica
description: Análisis sobre verificación de edad y la nueva legislación propuesta en Costa Rica para el control de uso de redes sociales en menores de edad.

title: ARGENSIG - Argentina School on Internet Governance
description: ARGENSIG trains future internet governance leaders across Latin America — onsite and hybrid — covering the technical, political, economic, social, and legal dimensions of the digital ecosystem, with a focus on the challenges of Argentina and the region.

title: South School on Internet Governance (SSIG) Guatemala 2026
description: SSIG Guatemala 2026 strengthens internet governance knowledge across Latin America and the Caribbean, covering cybersecurity, digital policy, infrastructure, and emerging technologies, promoting regional collaboration and participation in governance forums.
organizer: ISOC Guatemala Chapter, Centro de Capacitación para América Latina y el Caribe (CCATLAT), and Superintendencia de Telecomunicaciones (SIT)

title: Día Internacional de las Niñas en las TIC 2026 – Guatemala
description: International Girls in ICT Day 2026 in Guatemala inspires girls to explore technology careers through keynotes, interactive sessions, and discussions on AI, digital skills, and STEM education, promoting digital inclusion across Latin America and the Caribbean.
organizer: ISOC Guatemala Chapter, Superintendencia de Telecomunicaciones (SIT) y Ministerio de Comunicaciones, Infraestructura y Vivienda (MICIVI)

title: Guatemala Internet Governance Forum 2026 (IGF Guatemala 2026)
description: IGF Guatemala 2026 is a national multistakeholder platform uniting government, academia, private sector, civil society, and the technical community to discuss digital policy, cybersecurity, AI, and emerging technologies, promoting an inclusive internet ecosystem.
organizer: ISOC Guatemala Chapter, Guatemala Internet Governance Forum- Multistakeholder Community

title: Student Transition & Elevation Partnership Event
description: A networking and career-readiness event for engineering and tech students and young professionals, focused on preparing attendees for the transition from campus to career.
organizer: ISOC Trinidad and Tobago Chapter in collaboration with IEEE Young Professionals, CYICT and others

title: Más allá del acceso: ¿Qué significa ‘Internet significativo’ en educación?”
description: En Perú, la conectividad escolar ha crecido, pero no garantiza aprendizaje ni agencia digital. Este webinar explora qué condiciones pedagógicas, institucionales y tecnológicas convierten el acceso en capacidades reales.

title: Connecting Rural Suriname: Internet Access for Indigenous and Tribal Peoples for Distance Learning and Digital Inclusion
description: This project brings reliable internet access to rural Indigenous and Tribal communities in Suriname, supporting an existing distance learning programme through stable, safe connectivity to enable digital inclusion.

title: Campaña Neutralidad de la Red
description: Generaremos y pondremos a disposición del resto de los capítulos una campaña con una serie de productos gráficos (vídeos e ilustraciones) para generar conciencia sobre la importancia del principio de neutralidad de la red.

title: Conectividad Centrada en la Comunidad - - Internet segura
description: Este evento online enseña cómo mantener tu comunidad conectada de forma segura. Descubre herramientas prácticas para navegar con confianza y proteger tu información, con foco en los desafíos del despliegue de internet en comunidades urbanas y rurales.

title: Why Encryption Matters To You
description: This webinar will examine the importance of strong encryption tools for end users and will review potential encryption threats across select countries of the Caribbean.
organizer: ISOC Saint Vincent and the Grenadines Chapter and ISOC Trinidad and Tobago Chapter

title: Resiliencia de Internet ante Conflictos y Desastres
description: Internet es infraestructura crítica: ante desastres o conflictos, la conectividad puede ser vital. Este webinar explora los tres pilares de resiliencia de Internet Society para mantener redes operativas cuando más se necesitan.

title: Driving Sustainable Change: ISOC Haiti’s Community Network Model in Rivière Salée, Baradères.
description: Led by ISOC Haiti Chapter, this community network in Rivière Salée provides affordable internet to a rural community, supporting education, economic growth, and digital inclusion, sharing lessons learned to inform future initiatives.

title: Cifrado y Seguridad en Línea
description: "Si el cifrado es fuerte, Internet es fuerte". El objetivo de esta presentación es empoderar a la audiencia para que comprenda que el cifrado es la herramienta técnica que garantiza una sociedad digital democrática y segura.
organizer: ISOC Chile Chapter and the Facultad de Ingeniería y Ciencias UDP

title: CLNOG2026
description: Reunión de operadores de red de Chile.

title: Trust & Safety Online: Encryption and Internet Policy in the Caribbean
description: What do new online safety and age verification laws mean for your privacy? Join ISOC Jamaica for a conversation on encryption, the changing regulatory landscape, and how the LAC community can shape a safer internet.

title: Taller Online: Blindaje Digital – De las llaves perdidas a la seguridad total
description: ¿Cansado de contraseñas y preocupado por tu privacidad? En este taller gratuito aprenderemos a proteger tu vida digital de forma sencilla, sin importar tu edad.

title: Digital Rights & Online Safety Workshop 2026 "The Digital Rights & Online Safety Workshop"
description: A one-day in-person event (with online participation) bringing together 200 in-person participants, teachers, students, parents, school leaders, and NGOs,  to build practical skills in digital rights, online safety, cybersecurity, and child protection. Sessions address cyberbullying, online scams, misinformation, grooming, and privacy risks. The workshop closes with a Digital Safety Pledge and the launch of a Digital Safety Champions Network to sustain digital safety education in schools and communities.

title: Third Kenya National Community Networks Summit
description: A momentous event following summits in Siaya (2024) and Nairobi (2025), featuring a 3-day Pre-Summit Digital Security and AI Clinic (community networks only) and a 2-day main summit. Under the theme "Innovating for Grassroots Digital Access and Community Connectivity," the summit celebrates community-driven solutions — sharing best practices, fostering collaboration, and advocating for policies that support connectivity across Kenya.

title: AI, Internet Governance, and Bahrain’s Digital Future Webinar
description: This session will explore the evolving role of AI and internet governance in shaping Bahrain’s digital landscape.

title: Uganda Internet Governance Forum (UIGF)
description: The Uganda Internet Governance Forum (UIGF) is the nation's premier multi-stakeholder platform, uniting policymakers, innovators, and civil society since 2006 to shape Uganda's digital future across innovation, inclusion, cybersecurity, AI ethics, and digital safety.

title: Universal Acceptance Debate Cup
description: The Universal Acceptance Debate Cup brings together Harare high school students to debate multilingual internet access, digital inclusion, and internet governance, inspiring the next generation of digital leaders and policy advocates.

title: GAAD 2026: International Conference on Digital Accessibility, Inclusion and Innovation
description: Celebrate Global Accessibility Awareness Day 2026 with an interactive international conference bringing together global experts, policymakers, and accessibility champions to advocate for a digitally accessible world for everyone, everywhere.

title: Navigating Fragmented AI Rules: Mapping Global Digital Policy
description: Experts from industry, academia, and policy come together to explore the challenges and implications of an increasingly fragmented AI regulatory landscape, as governments around the world race to regulate artificial intelligence.

title: ISOC Community Network Infrastructure SIG Day at Wireless Community Weekend
description: The Community Network Infrastructure SIG, organizes Workshops, Talks, and BBQ Reception during the Wirless Community Weekend in Berlin.  Join our in person mapping workshop of community networks: Challenges, Learnings and Perspectives and join us for the BBQ Reception!
